predecessor: baseline-round4-2026-07-20.json (round 4, against 345ddcc, before the 'er með' cue and the methodology-field extractor)
method: Round 4's next-steps pointed at the chart-vs-prose completeness check as the longest-standing open item, and the handoff explicitly called for round 5 to alternate back to close-reading individual articles (rounds 1/2/4's method) rather than round 3's batch/scale approach. Close-read the chart-vs-prose completeness question directly (12 real chart-bearing articles, diffed chart parties against independently-run prose extraction on the same paragraphs) and found it clean — but the same close-reading swept far wider than planned: ~38 of the ~40 currently-listed RÚV poll articles were fetched and manually inspected for extraction correctness, well beyond the core 6-article regression set. That sweep surfaced three real false positives, all from the same previously-unrecognized failure class (RÚV's 'skoðanakönnun' tag also covers leader-trust/job-approval/satisfaction polls, which share poll-cue vocabulary with genuine party-support polls but aren't about party support at all), fixed with two new guards. It also surfaced a real, previously-untestable cross-source methodology comparison (round 4's Q25) via cross_reference_duplicates()'s automatic RÚV/Vísir pairing, plus a new deliberately-unfixed verb-mood gap and a newly-characterized (not fixed) methodology-only-article gap.
headline_findings: Three real false positives found and fixed, all one failure class: RÚV's 'skoðanakönnun' tag also catches leader-trust, job-approval, and satisfaction polls (not party-fylgi polls at all), which share exact poll-cue vocabulary ('mælist', 'stendur') with genuine party-support sentences. ruv-458088 (trust in ministers), ruv-453144 (taxi-market satisfaction by voter subgroup), and ruv-458497 (leader job-approval) each produced one-to-two bogus party-support rows via stale current_party carryover from a party mention that was never actually about that party's own support level (a chairperson title, a voter-subgroup breakdown, an idiom collision on 'stendur sig'). Two guards close all three: _NON_SUPPORT_TOPIC_RE (skip a sentence outright if its own topic is satisfaction/trust/approval/voter-subgroup framing, regardless of cue verb) and a tightened current_party fallback (a trend-cue-only sentence with no in-sentence party now also requires an actual poll-cue verb before inheriting current_party). Verified against the full 6-article regression set: identical party counts to round 4 (8, 7, 9, 10, 6, 6) and identical pytest results (119 passed, 6 skipped, 1 xfailed).

The chart-vs-prose completeness check (open since round 3, round 4's #1 next-step) is now closed: ran independent prose extraction against the same paragraphs on 12 real chart-bearing RÚV articles spanning Nov 2024-Jun 2026, diffed against each article's own chart party set, and found zero cases of a chart silently missing a party the prose corroborates. Chart extraction is not a completeness risk on this sample.

Cross-source methodology comparison (round 4's Q25, 'unable' — no testable pair existed) is now answerable, with a genuinely positive result: cross_reference_duplicates() already links 5 real RÚV/Vísir pairs covering the same poll. One pair (ruv-468519 / visir-20262850764) has methodology on both sides, and the numbers agree exactly (sample_size=9958, response_rate_pct=44.3 on both). This is the first real end-to-end evidence that extract_methodology() produces consistent numbers across independently-written coverage of the same underlying poll, not just internally-consistent numbers within one article.

That same cross-source check surfaced a new, real, unfixed gap: visir-20262850764 (the Vísir half of the pair above) extracts zero party-support rows (source='none') despite having valid methodology — and cmd_fetch's `if not result['parties']: continue` guard means this article's raw JSON is never written and its methodology never reaches the CSV at all. The only reason this round could see it was calling fetch_visir_article() directly rather than through cmd_fetch. Methodology is effectively invisible for any article that has real aðferðafræði prose but zero extractable party figures — a real, previously uncharacterized scope gap, left unfixed this round (a schema/pipeline change, not a regex fix, and out of scope for a same-day extension of this round).

A second verb-mood gap was found and deliberately left unfixed, same discipline as the round-3/4 cue-verb additions: article 427650's 'Báðir flokkarnir slyppu naumlega inn á þing með um 5,5 prósenta fylgi' and 'Píratar féllu af þingi með 3,4%' (electoral-threshold framing — 'would (barely) make it into/fall out of parliament') are genuine current-poll figures that neither _POLL_CUE_RE nor _TREND_CUE_RE recognizes. Single-article evidence only, so per this skill's evidence-before-cue rule it stays a documented gap rather than a new regex addition.

results.answer: Three: ruv-458088 ('Kristrún nýtur mests trausts en flestir vantreysta Guðmundi Inga'), ruv-453144 ('Yfirgnæfandi óánægja með leigubílamarkaðinn'), ruv-458497 ('Langmest ánægja með störf Kristrúnar') — found across a ~38-article sweep of the current RÚV tag listing, not the 6-article regression set.

All three are RÚV 'skoðanakönnun'-tagged articles that are NOT party-support polls at all (trust in ministers, taxi-market satisfaction by voter subgroup, leader job-approval), but each contains at least one sentence using a real _POLL_CUE_RE verb ('mælist' or 'stendur') whose grammatical subject is something other than a party's support figure, combined with a party name set as current_party by an earlier, unrelated sentence (a chairperson title, a voter-subgroup mention) — producing a bogus party-support row via pronoun carryover.

(1) _NON_SUPPORT_TOPIC_RE — matches ánægj*/óánægj*/traust*/vantreyst*/staðið sig/fylgismann*/kjósend*/kusu/kaus, skips the whole sentence outright regardless of cue verb, checked immediately after a sentence is confirmed to have a percent match. (2) The current_party pronoun-fallback branch (used when a sentence has no in-sentence party match and isn't an aggregate) now also requires an actual _POLL_CUE_RE match, not just a _TREND_CUE_RE match, before inheriting current_party — a trend-cue-only sentence with neither an in-sentence party nor a poll verb is now skipped instead of guessed.

Because ruv-453144's worst sentence ('Minnst mælist hún í röðum fylgismanna Vinstri grænna, 61 prósent.') has BOTH an in-sentence party match ('Vinstri grænna') AND a real poll-cue verb ('mælist') — it goes through the direct in-sentence-party-attribution path, not the current_party fallback path, so guard #2 (the poll-cue-verb requirement on fallback) never applies to it at all. Only _NON_SUPPORT_TOPIC_RE's 'fylgismann*' term catches this specific sentence; a second sentence in the same article ('Það er eini hópurinn... 10,1 prósenti') needed the topic-word part of the same regex ('ánægjan' isn't restated in that sentence, but current_party carried 'Píratar' from an earlier 'meðal Pírata' sentence which itself has no percent to trigger on).

Not touched directly — 'stendur' remains in _POLL_CUE_RE unchanged (still needed for genuine 'flokkurinn stendur í X prósentum' constructions). The general _NON_SUPPORT_TOPIC_RE guard resolved the 'stendur sig' idiom collision indirectly: the false-positive sentence itself ('Sigurður Ingi stendur sig litlu betur, 58 prósent segja hann hafa staðið sig illa') contains 'staðið sig', which the topic guard matches and skips outright, so 'stendur' never needed a lookahead exclusion for 'sig'.

Yes — all three re-fetched live via `uv run python scripts/skodanakannanir.py fetch <id>` after the fix (raw JSON deleted first to force a fresh scrape), not just checked against a hand-written regex test: ruv-458088 and ruv-458497 now extract 0 parties (correctly, 'no chart and no prose figures extracted'), ruv-453144 dropped from 2 bogus parties to 0.

Yes — all six regression articles (451831, 428434, 467932, 468092, visir-20262884571, visir-20262904348) were re-fetched after both guards were added and produced identical party counts to round 4 (8, 7, 9, 10, 6, 6), confirming none of their real party-support sentences use any word in the new guard's vocabulary.

By systematic breadth testing, not targeted search — the chart-vs-prose completeness check (this round's planned task) required fetching a dozen-plus real articles, which expanded naturally into fetching nearly every article on the current RÚV tag listing (many with titles suggesting non-party-support topics: Eurovision, whale hunting, energy drinks, Shein purchases) specifically to see whether the extractor stayed silent on genuinely unrelated polls — three of them weren't silent.

Yes, finally executed this round — 12 real chart-bearing RÚV articles checked, chart parties diffed against independent prose extraction on the same paragraphs. Zero gaps found: no article's chart silently missed a party its own prose corroborated with a real poll-cue sentence.

12 articles (ruv-479261, 470704, 468519, 468092, 467932, 465942, 464439, 456759, 454256, 447033, 434441, 427475), spanning first_published_at dates from 2024-11-14 to 2026-06-24 — a year and a half of real chart-bearing coverage, not a handful of adjacent articles.

No — zero such cases across the 12-article sample. The only 'in prose, not chart' hit in the raw diff (ruv-458088) turned out to be a zero-bar article (no chart at all, already correctly routed to prose-fallback), not a real chart-completeness gap, and is now moot anyway since ruv-458088's prose extraction itself was fixed to 0 parties this round (see Q1-2).

Considered closed for now — the 12-article sample spans 19 months of real coverage with a clean result, a materially stronger evidence bar than the one-off spot checks this item was flagged against since round 3. Not proven for all time (a chart embedding change or an unusual chart layout could still surface a gap later), but no longer the single longest-standing open item.
Yes — ruv-468519 / visir-20262850764, both covering the same poll (matched by cross_reference_duplicates()'s pollster+scope+48h-window rule), have methodology populated on both sides.
Yes, exactly — sample_size=9958 and response_rate_pct=44.3 on both the RÚV and the Vísir side, extracted independently from two differently-written articles about the same underlying poll.

Via the existing automatic pairing — `list --source all` already runs cross_reference_duplicates() and had linked 5 real RÚV/Vísir pairs (visir-20262859852→ruv-470704, visir-20262850764→ruv-468519, visir-20262847801→ruv-467932, visir-20262838042→ruv-465942, visir-20262832055→ruv-464439) sitting in articles.json; this round fetched all 10 articles (5 pairs) to check methodology on both sides rather than building any new matching logic.

Yes, a real gap: visir-20262850764 (the methodology-bearing article in the matched pair) extracts zero party rows through fetch_visir_article(), and cmd_fetch's `if not result['parties']: continue` guard skips writing its raw JSON entirely before methodology ever gets a chance to reach the CSV — the only reason this round could see its methodology at all was calling fetch_visir_article() directly, bypassing cmd_fetch. See Q19-20.

Yes — article 427650's 'Báðir flokkarnir slyppu naumlega inn á þing með um 5,5 prósenta fylgi hvor flokkur og þrjá menn hvor' (Viðreisn and Samfylking, both projected to barely clear the threshold) and 'Píratar féllu af þingi með 3,4%, og eins Vinstri grænir með 2,4 af hundraði' — neither 'slyppu' nor 'féllu' is in _POLL_CUE_RE, so both sentences were logged '[no poll cue]' and skipped, silently dropping 3 real current-poll figures (Samfylking, Píratar, Vinstri græn) from that one article.

Deliberately left unfixed — logged as a documented gap in SKILL.md, not added to _POLL_CUE_RE, because it's evidenced by only one article so far. Per this skill's own rule (every prior cue addition needed 2-3 independent real examples first), a single occurrence isn't enough evidence yet.

Yes, a real (not hypothetical) case: visir-20262850764 has sample_size=9958 and response_rate_pct=44.3 from extract_methodology(), verified by calling fetch_visir_article() directly, but result['parties'] is empty (source='none'), so cmd_fetch's early continue means this article's {id}.json is never written and none of its methodology data reaches data/processed/skodanakannanir.csv.

Documented only, not fixed this round — it's a pipeline/schema change (methodology is currently stored per-party-support-row, denormalized, so an article with zero party rows has structurally nowhere to put its methodology in the current CSV shape), not a regex fix, and wasn't planned or scoped for this round. Flagged in highest_value_next_steps below.
Yes — `uv run pytest -m "not slow" -q` returned '119 passed, 6 skipped, 146 deselected, 1 xfailed', identical pass/skip/xfail counts to round 4, run fresh after both this round's guards were added.

Yes, unchanged: 451831=8, 428434=7, 467932=9, 468092=10, visir-20262884571=6, visir-20262904348=6 — every count identical to round 4's baseline, confirming neither new guard touched any real verified party-support sentence.

Around 38 of the ~40 RÚV articles currently on the tag listing were fetched and inspected this round (2 timed out transiently and weren't retried: ruv-467641, ruv-455809) — the widest single-round sweep so far, well beyond the 6-article core regression set.

No — Heimildin fetch (number extraction) remains fully unbuilt this round; only discovery exists. This round's investigative effort went into the chart-vs-prose completeness check and the false positives it surfaced instead.

No new VB work this round — still fully unstarted across all five rounds. Deliberately deprioritized again: this round's close-reading approach surfaced three real correctness bugs on the already-shipped RÚV/Vísir paths, and per this skill's own stated priority (a wrong number on a working source is worse than a missing source), fixing those took precedence over starting a new source.

Yes, unchanged — the try/except around each article's fetch call in cmd_fetch (round 3's fix) wasn't touched by this round's changes, which only modified extract_prose_poll_figures()'s internal sentence-level logic and SKILL.md documentation.

One timeout occurred (ruv-467641, 'Page.goto: Timeout 60000ms exceeded') during this round's ~38-article sweep — but it's the exact, already-documented failure mode round 3's try/except was built to survive (the batch continued past it without crashing, per Q26), not a new problem. A second article (ruv-455809) also failed the same way and also wasn't investigated further since neither article's title suggests party-support content worth retrying for.
results.gap: Neither ruv-467641 nor ruv-455809 was successfully fetched this round, so their extraction correctness (whether they'd also turn out to be non-support-topic false positives, or genuine unfetched party-support articles) is unverified.

results.answer: The #1 item (chart-vs-prose completeness check) was fully resolved. The #2 item (cross-source methodology comparison, round 4's Q25) went from 'unable, no testable pair exists' to answered with a positive result. The #3 item (VB discovery) was not started, consistently deprioritized for a third round running.

Yes — every bug found this round (the three non-support-topic false positives, the conditional-mood gap, the methodology-only-article gap) came from reading real individual articles end-to-end, the same method rounds 1/2/4 used, not from round 3's --all-batch/scale approach. The close-reading did end up covering unusually wide breadth (~38 articles) as a side effect of the chart-vs-prose completeness check's sample size requirement, but the bug-finding method itself was still close-reading, not batch scale-testing.

results.gap: No single clear largest-remaining-item the way round 4 had one — three roughly comparable gaps remain open: (1) the methodology-only-article pipeline gap (Q19-20, newly characterized this round, needs a schema change), (2) VB discovery (unstarted for five rounds running, largest scope gap), (3) Heimildin fetch (discovery-only). Listing all three in highest_value_next_steps rather than forcing a single answer — this round didn't produce evidence that clearly ranks one above the others the way the chart-vs-prose check was unambiguously the top item after round 3.

gap_patterns_round5: The three non-support-topic false positives were found purely as a side effect of doing the planned chart-vs-prose completeness check thoroughly (which needed a wide, real sample of articles) rather than from a targeted search for this bug class — a reminder that breadth-for-a-different-purpose is still one of the more reliable ways this eval loop finds new bugs, alongside round 3's explicit batch/scale testing.

This round found a second instance of the same meta-pattern round 4's Q16 caught: 'verify the specific claim, don't just describe the general shape of it.' Here it showed up as cmd_fetch silently discarding valid methodology data for zero-party articles — a gap invisible unless you call the extraction function directly and compare against what actually reaches the CSV, not just checking that extract_methodology() itself works in isolation.
highest_value_next_steps: Fix the methodology-only-article gap (Q16, Q19-20, newly characterized this round): cmd_fetch currently drops any article with zero extracted party rows before its methodology ever reaches data/processed/skodanakannanir.csv or even a raw {id}.json. Needs an article-level methodology record independent of the per-party-row CSV shape, not just a regex fix.
VB discovery remains completely unstarted across all five rounds — still the largest single scope gap in the skill, deliberately deprioritized behind correctness work three rounds running.

The conditional-mood cue-verb gap ('slyppu inn á þing'/'féllu af þingi', Q17-18) is documented but unfixed — needs a second independent real example before it can be added to _POLL_CUE_RE, per this skill's evidence bar.
